title: Door Lock Fault
shortDescription: The door interlock is not engaging or the door microswitch is not providing a closed signal.
fullDescription: Error E01 on Candy washing machines indicates the door lock (interlock) mechanism has not engaged within the expected time. The machine cannot start a cycle without confirming the door is securely locked. This is common on Candy and Hoover machines which share the same platform.

causes.cause: Door not fully closed or catch misaligned
Control board door lock output failed

symptoms: E01 error code or 1 flash on LED
Door will not lock when cycle started
Door lock clicks but does not hold

fixSteps.title: Check door closure
fixSteps.instruction: Push the door firmly until you hear a solid click. Check nothing is preventing full closure — clothing, door seal, or a damaged catch.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door catch and striker
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door catch and the striker plate on the machine body. Replace if worn or broken.

fixSteps.title: Test the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Access the interlock from inside the door rim. Disconnect and test continuity across the terminals when mechanically engaged. The interlock should show continuity when locked.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Peel back the door rubber seal, remove the retaining spring or band, and extract the interlock. Install the new unit, refit the seal and band.

whenToCallPro: If the interlock and catch are fine, the control board may have a faulty output relay.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E01 door lock interlock fix

faq.q: How do I open a stuck door?
faq.a: Wait 2-3 minutes after turning off the machine. If still locked, use the emergency release — a small loop or tab accessible behind the filter panel.

faq.q: Are Candy and Hoover error codes the same?
faq.a: Yes, Candy and Hoover share the same electronic platform. E01 means the same door lock fault on both brands.

faq.q: Can I bypass the door lock?
faq.a: Never bypass the interlock. It prevents the door opening during high-speed spin, which is a serious safety risk.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The washer has not filled with water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E02 indicates the machine did not detect the required water level within the fill timeout period. This is typically a water supply problem rather than a machine fault. The fill valves opened but insufficient water reached the tub.

causes.cause: Water supply tap turned off or not fully open
Inlet filter screens clogged with sediment

symptoms: E02 error code or 2 LED flashes
Fill valve buzzes but no water flows

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water supply tap is fully open. Turn it off and on to confirm flow.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose and filters
fixSteps.instruction: Check the inlet hose for kinks. Disconnect from the machine and clean the mesh filter screens inside the hose connectors and valve inlets.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: During a fill attempt, check for 230V at the valve connector. If voltage is present but no water flows, the valve is stuck closed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply, disconnect hose, remove valve mounting screws and electrical connector. Install new valve.

whenToCallPro: Only if the inlet valve needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E02 water fill timeout error fix

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause E02?
faq.a: Yes, if home water pressure is below about 1 bar, the machine may not fill fast enough.

faq.q: Do I need both hot and cold connections?
faq.a: Most modern Candy washers only use cold fill. Check your model manual.

faq.q: How often should I clean the inlet filters?
faq.a: Every 6 months, or more frequently if you have hard water with sediment.

title: Drain Timeout / Pump Fault
shortDescription: The washer could not drain the water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E03 indicates the machine failed to drain water from the tub within the expected time. The drain pump may be blocked, the drain hose obstructed, or the pump motor has failed. Standing water will remain in the drum.

causes.cause: Drain pump filter clogged with debris

symptoms: E03 error code or 3 LED flashes
Drain pump sounds strained or silent

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the access panel at the bottom-front. Place towels and a shallow tray below. Slowly unscrew the filter cap, let water drain, and remove any debris.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull machine from wall and inspect drain hose for kinks. Disconnect from standpipe and check for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With filter removed, look inside the pump housing and check if the impeller turns freely. Remove any glass, coins, or debris.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump motor is dead, disconnect electrical connector and hose clamps, remove mounting screws, and install new pump.

whenToCallPro: If the pump has failed and you are not comfortable replacing it.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E03 drain pump blocked error fix

faq.q: How do I manually drain the water?
faq.a: Open the filter panel, place towels and container below, slowly unscrew the filter cap to let water drain.

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: Every 1-2 months is recommended.

faq.q: Can the sink drain cause E03?
faq.a: Yes, a blocked sink waste pipe prevents the dishwasher from draining properly.

title: Overfill / Water Level Too High
shortDescription: The water level in the tub has exceeded the maximum safe level.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates the water level sensor has detected the water level is too high. The machine may have an inlet valve stuck open, a faulty pressure switch, or the anti-siphon on the drain hose is not working. This is a safety fault to prevent flooding.

causes.cause: Drain hose siphoning water back into machine

symptoms: E04 error code or 4 LED flashes
Water level visibly too high in drum

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately close the water supply tap. Unplug the machine.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose routing
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the drain hose has a high loop (above drum level) before going down to the standpipe. Without this, water can siphon back into the machine.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the machine unplugged and water supply on, check if water still flows into the machine. If it does, the valve is stuck open.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve leaks when unpowered, replace it. Turn off water, disconnect hose, swap valve.

whenToCallPro: If valve and drain hose are fine, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E04 overfill water level error fix

faq.q: Can E04 cause flooding?
faq.a: Yes, turn off the water supply immediately.

faq.q: What is siphoning?
faq.a: When the drain hose is pushed too far into the standpipe without a high loop, water can be sucked back into the machine by gravity.

faq.q: Should I turn off the tap when not using the washer?
faq.a: It is good practice, especially on older machines, to prevent flooding from valve failures.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor is providing an out-of-range reading.
fullDescription: Error E05 indicates the NTC thermistor is open, shorted, or reading outside its expected range. Without valid temperature data, the washer cannot heat water safely. On some Candy models, E05 may also indicate the heater circuit is faulty.

causes.cause: NTC wiring connector loose or corroded
Heating element has a ground fault

symptoms: E05 error code or 5 LED flashes
Water does not heat during wash
Clothes come out cold on hot wash

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC in the heater element housing. Disconnect and measure resistance — approximately 20K ohms at 20°C room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Measure heater resistance (20-30 ohms) and insulation to ground (over 2 megaohms). A leaking heater can affect the NTC circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace NTC or heater
fixSteps.instruction: Replace whichever component tested faulty. The NTC pulls out of the heater housing easily.

whenToCallPro: Only if NTC, heater, and wiring all test good.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E05 NTC temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Can I wash with E05?
faq.a: The machine may run on some models but will not heat water. Cold washing is possible but not ideal.

faq.q: Is the NTC inside the heater?
faq.a: On most Candy models, the NTC is a separate probe in the heater housing.

faq.q: What does NTC stand for?
faq.a: Negative Temperature Coefficient — a type of thermistor whose resistance decreases as temperature increases.

title: EEPROM / Memory Error
shortDescription: The EEPROM memory on the control board has a data corruption error.
fullDescription: Error E06 indicates the EEPROM chip storing cycle programs and configuration has corrupted data. The machine cannot run programs correctly. Usually caused by power interruptions or voltage spikes.
symptoms: E06 error code or 6 LED flashes
Machine will not start any cycle

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes for a complete capacitor discharge. Reconnect and try again.

fixSteps.title: Reseat board connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Open the top panel and reseat all connectors on the control board firmly.

fixSteps.title: EEPROM reprogramming
fixSteps.instruction: A technician may reprogram the EEPROM using a diagnostic tool with the correct firmware.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If reprogramming fails, replace with a pre-programmed board.

whenToCallPro: E06 almost always requires professional intervention.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E06 EEPROM memory error fix

faq.q: What causes EEPROM corruption?
faq.a: Power outages during write operations, voltage spikes, and aging.

faq.q: Can a power cut cause E06?
faq.a: Yes, especially if the power fails during a cycle while the board is writing data.

faq.q: Is the board pre-programmed?
faq.a: Genuine replacement boards come with the correct firmware for the model range.

title: Motor Fault / Tacho Error
shortDescription: The motor tachometer is not providing a valid speed signal to the control board.
fullDescription: Error E07 indicates the control board is not receiving the expected speed feedback from the motor tachometer. This prevents the board from controlling drum speed safely. Worn carbon brushes are the most common cause on Candy machines with brushed motors.
symptoms: E07 error code or 7 LED flashes
Motor hums but drum does not move

fixSteps.title: Reset and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and try a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check carbon brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel and extract the carbon brushes from either side of the motor. If shorter than 1 cm, replace them.

fixSteps.title: Test the tachometer
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the tacho coil (100-200 ohms). Check the magnet is secure on the motor shaft.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor windings
fixSteps.instruction: Measure motor winding resistance (2-8 ohms). Check for ground faults between windings and housing.

whenToCallPro: If brushes and tacho are fine, the motor or control board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E07 motor carbon brushes tacho fix

faq.q: How long do carbon brushes last?
faq.a: Typically 5-8 years with normal use.

faq.q: Can I replace brushes myself?
faq.a: Yes, carbon brush replacement is one of the most common DIY washer repairs.

faq.q: Do newer Candy washers have brushless motors?
faq.a: Some newer models use brushless inverter motors which do not have carbon brushes. E07 on those models indicates a different motor circuit fault.

title: Motor Triac Fault
shortDescription: The motor triac on the control board has short-circuited or failed.
fullDescription: Error E08 indicates the triac semiconductor that controls power to the motor has failed. The motor may run uncontrolled or not at all. This is a control board component fault.

causes.cause: Motor triac has failed on control board
Motor short circuit damaged the triac

symptoms: E08 error code or 8 LED flashes

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. If E08 returns, the board likely needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor windings for shorts and ground faults. A shorted motor can destroy the triac.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor wiring for damage, chafing, or shorts to ground.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is fine, the triac has failed. Replace the control board. Also replace the motor if it tested faulty.

whenToCallPro: E08 requires control board replacement. Professional service recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E08 motor triac control board fix

faq.q: Can the triac be replaced individually?
faq.a: Technically yes with electronics soldering, but full board replacement is standard.

faq.q: Will a surge protector prevent E08?
faq.a: It helps protect against voltage spikes which can damage triacs.

faq.q: Is E08 the same on Hoover machines?
faq.a: Yes, Candy and Hoover share the same platform. E08 means the same fault.

title: Motor Triac Short Circuit
shortDescription: The motor triac is confirmed stuck in the on position.
fullDescription: Error E09 specifically indicates the motor triac is shorted (stuck on), meaning the motor receives continuous uncontrolled power. This is a safety error that halts the machine immediately. E09 is more specific than E08.

causes.cause: Motor short circuit caused triac damage

symptoms: E09 error code or 9 LED flashes
Motor runs at full speed uncontrollably

fixSteps.title: Unplug immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is running uncontrolled, unplug the machine immediately for safety.

fixSteps.title: Test motor for shorts
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor windings and insulation. A short-circuited motor can cause the triac to fail.

fixSteps.title: Inspect board
fixSteps.instruction: Check the control board for visible damage around the triac area.

fixSteps.title: Replace board and motor if needed
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the control board. If motor also failed, replace both.

whenToCallPro: E09 is safety-critical. Professional repair strongly recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E09 motor triac short circuit fix

faq.q: Is E09 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, uncontrolled motor operation is a safety hazard. Unplug immediately.

faq.q: What is the difference between E08 and E09?
faq.a: E08 is a general triac fault. E09 specifically confirms the triac is stuck on (shorted).

faq.q: Can I still use the machine?
faq.a: Absolutely not. Do not use the machine until the fault is repaired.

title: Selector / Pressure Switch Error
shortDescription: The program selector or the pressure switch is providing conflicting signals.
fullDescription: Error E10 indicates a conflict between the program selector position and the pressure switch reading, or a general pressure switch error depending on the model. The control board cannot determine the correct operating state.

causes.cause: Pressure switch hose blocked or disconnected

symptoms: E10 error code or 10 LED flashes
Machine fills but does not progress

fixSteps.title: Check pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the small rubber hose from tub to pressure switch is connected and not blocked.

fixSteps.title: Test pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and blow into the hose port. Listen for clicks indicating the switch changes state.

fixSteps.title: Check program selector
fixSteps.instruction: If pressure switch is fine, check the program selector dial contacts for wear or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the pressure switch or selector as needed.

whenToCallPro: If both the pressure switch and selector test fine.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E10 pressure switch selector error fix

faq.q: What is the program selector?
faq.a: The dial or electronic switch used to select wash cycles. On some models it is mechanical; on others it is digital.

faq.q: Can vibration cause E10?
faq.a: Yes, vibration can disconnect the pressure hose or loosen the selector contacts.

faq.q: Is E10 common?
faq.a: Less common than drain or door lock errors. It usually indicates a specific sensor fault.

title: Dryer Circuit Fault (Washer-Dryer)
shortDescription: The drying circuit is not functioning on washer-dryer models.
fullDescription: Error E11 on Candy washer-dryer combos indicates a fault in the drying heating element or its circuit. The dryer heater may be open circuit, the thermal fuse may have tripped, or the control board cannot power the dryer heater.

fixSteps.title: Reset and check thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 15 minutes. The thermal fuse may reset after cooling.

fixSteps.title: Test dryer heater
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance (20-40 ohms). Open circuit means element failed.

fixSteps.title: Test thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Check for continuity. If open, replace.

fixSteps.title: Replace failed component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace heater or thermal fuse as needed.

whenToCallPro: If heater and fuse test good, the board dryer relay may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer dryer E11 heater element fix

faq.q: Does E11 only affect drying?
faq.a: Yes, the washing function continues to work normally.

faq.q: Why does the thermal fuse trip?
faq.a: Blocked lint filter, restricted airflow, or overloading the dryer.

faq.q: Can I use just the washing function?
faq.a: Yes, wash-only cycles should work fine with E11.

title: Display Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication between the display module and main control board has failed.
fullDescription: Error E12 indicates a communication fault between the display board and the main control board. The boards are typically connected by a ribbon cable or wiring harness. A loose connection, failed board, or moisture damage can cause this.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open the top panel and firmly reseat the cable between display and main board.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect boards for moisture or corrosion. Clean with electronics contact cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes after reseating. Test again.

fixSteps.title: Replace display board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating fails, replace the display board first (less expensive).

whenToCallPro: If unsure which board is faulty after reseating.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E12 display communication error fix

faq.q: Can steam cause E12?
faq.a: Yes, steam from overloading or excessive detergent can damage board connectors.

faq.q: Which board to replace first?
faq.a: Start with the cable, then the display board.

faq.q: Is this common?
faq.a: Moderately common, especially on machines in humid laundry rooms.

title: General Communication Error
shortDescription: A general communication failure has occurred between system components.
fullDescription: Error E13 is a general communication error indicating multiple or unspecified communication failures between the machine's electronic modules. It can encompass display, motor control, and sensor communication faults.

causes.cause: Multiple loose connectors on control board

symptoms: E13 error code or 13 LED flashes
Display flickers or shows random data

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes. This clears all volatile states.

fixSteps.title: Check all connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Open the machine and reseat every connector on the control board and sensor wiring.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure stable mains voltage (230V). Try a different outlet. Avoid sharing the circuit with high-draw appliances.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If E13 persists after all checks, the control board has likely partially failed.

whenToCallPro: If connector reseating and power checks do not resolve E13.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E13 communication error fix

faq.q: Is E13 serious?
faq.a: It can be. If a simple reset does not clear it, professional diagnosis is recommended.

faq.q: Can a faulty outlet cause E13?
faq.a: Yes, unstable power supply can cause communication glitches between electronic modules.

faq.q: Should I use a surge protector?
faq.a: Yes, a surge protector helps prevent voltage-related communication errors.

title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The wash heating element has failed or is not being powered.
fullDescription: Error E14 indicates the wash heating element circuit has a fault. The element may be open circuit (burned out), shorted to ground, or the control board cannot supply power to it. Water will not be heated during wash cycles.
symptoms: E14 error code or 14 LED flashes
Water stays cold on hot wash

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. If E14 returns, test the heater.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect heater terminals and measure resistance (20-30 ohms). Check insulation to ground (over 2 megaohms).

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect heater terminal connections for corrosion or heat damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace heater
fixSteps.instruction: If failed, remove the old element from the tub and install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the heater trips the RCD or if the control board relay is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E14 heating element replacement

faq.q: Can I wash with cold water?
faq.a: The machine will run but cleaning will be poor, especially for greasy or stained items.

faq.q: Why does the RCD trip?
faq.a: A heater leaking electricity to ground causes the safety switch to trip.

faq.q: How long does a heater last?
faq.a: Typically 7-12 years. Hard water areas may see earlier failure from limescale buildup.

title: Heater Relay Fault
shortDescription: The heater relay on the control board is stuck or malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error E15 indicates the relay controlling the heating element is not functioning correctly. It may be stuck on (causing overheating) or stuck off (no heat). This is a control board fault.

causes.cause: Heater relay welded shut or open

symptoms: E15 error code or 15 LED flashes

fixSteps.title: Unplug if overheating
fixSteps.instruction: If water is overheating or steam visible, unplug immediately.

fixSteps.title: Test heater
fixSteps.instruction: Check resistance and insulation. A faulty heater can cause relay damage.

fixSteps.title: Inspect relay
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burnt or discolored relay on the control board.

fixSteps.title: Replace board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the control board. Also replace heater if faulty.

whenToCallPro: E15 involves safety-critical components. Professional repair recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E15 heater relay fix

faq.q: Is E15 dangerous?
faq.a: If the relay is stuck on, overheating is possible. Unplug immediately.

faq.q: Can I replace just the relay?
faq.a: Full board replacement is standard and more reliable.

faq.q: What causes relay failure?
faq.a: Aging, heater short circuits, and power surges.

title: Heater Short Circuit
shortDescription: The heating element is confirmed to have a short circuit.
fullDescription: Error E16 indicates the control board has detected a short circuit condition in the heating element. The heater is leaking current to ground, which is a safety hazard. The machine will not operate until the heater is replaced.

causes.cause: Heating element insulation has broken down
Water ingress into heater terminal housing

symptoms: E16 error code or 16 LED flashes
Machine will not start or stops immediately
E16 appears on any cycle including cold

fixSteps.title: Test heater insulation
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect heater and test insulation resistance between each terminal and the heater body. Must exceed 2 megaohms. Below 1 megaohm indicates a developing fault.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the heater terminal block for water ingress. Dry thoroughly. Sometimes condensation causes temporary insulation breakdown.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check heater wiring for damaged insulation that could cause a short to the machine frame.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heater
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old element and install replacement with new seal.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable testing insulation resistance near mains voltage components.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E16 heater short circuit fix

faq.q: Why does the RCD keep tripping?
faq.a: The heater is leaking electricity to ground. This is detected by the RCD and trips it for safety.

faq.q: Can limescale cause E16?
faq.a: Yes, limescale buildup on the heater element can crack the protective coating, allowing water to reach the heating wire and cause a ground fault.

faq.q: How can I prevent heater failure?
faq.a: Use a limescale prevention product regularly, especially in hard water areas.

title: Drum Position Sensor Error
shortDescription: The drum position or speed sensor is not providing valid data.
fullDescription: Error E17 indicates the drum position sensor or the motor hall sensor is not providing the expected signal. On models with direct-drive or inverter motors, this sensor helps the control board track the exact drum position for optimal wash action.

causes.cause: Hall sensor on motor has failed
Control board sensor input circuit failed

symptoms: E17 error code or 17 LED flashes

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. Sensor glitches can sometimes clear.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor and ensure the hall sensor connector is firmly plugged in.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure sensor resistance and check for signal output when the motor shaft is rotated by hand.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor has failed, remove and replace it. On direct-drive motors, the sensor is typically a separate module.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests fine, the motor or control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E17 hall sensor drum position error fix

faq.q: What is a hall sensor?
faq.a: A hall sensor detects the magnetic field of the motor rotor to determine its position and speed.

faq.q: Does E17 only apply to inverter motors?
faq.a: Primarily yes. Machines with traditional brushed motors use a tachometer instead.

faq.q: Can overloading cause E17?
faq.a: In rare cases, extreme overloading can stress the motor and cause erratic sensor readings.

title: Load Imbalance / Vibration Error
shortDescription: The machine has detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle.
fullDescription: Error E18 indicates the control board detected excessive vibration or imbalance during the spin cycle. The machine attempts to redistribute the load by tumbling at low speed, but if the imbalance persists, the spin cycle is aborted to prevent damage or movement of the machine.

causes.cause: Laundry load is unevenly distributed in the drum
Single heavy item (towel, duvet) creating imbalance
Shock absorbers or dampers are worn
Drum suspension springs are broken or detached

symptoms: E18 error code or 18 LED flashes
Machine walks or moves across floor

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and redistribute the laundry evenly around the drum. If washing a single large item, add a few smaller items for balance.

fixSteps.title: Check machine level
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all four feet are firmly on the floor and the machine is level. Adjust the feet as needed. Lock the locking nuts.

fixSteps.title: Inspect shock absorbers
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the machine or access from underneath. Press down on the drum and release — it should bounce once and settle. Excessive bouncing means worn absorbers.

fixSteps.title: Replace shock absorbers
fixSteps.instruction: If absorbers are worn, replace them. They are typically held by pins or bolts at both ends.

whenToCallPro: If redistributing the load does not help and the machine is level, the suspension system may need professional inspection.
videoSearchQuery: Candy washer E18 vibration imbalance error fix

faq.q: Is E18 always a fault?
faq.a: Not necessarily. It often indicates an unbalanced load rather than a machine fault. Try redistributing the laundry first.

faq.q: How do I prevent E18?
faq.a: Avoid washing single large items alone. Mix large and small items for better balance. Do not overload the drum.

faq.q: Can worn shock absorbers cause E18?
faq.a: Yes, worn absorbers allow excessive drum movement, triggering the imbalance sensor even with a balanced load.
